text.title: NoSQL Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real NoSQL deployments. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team picks MongoDB "because microservices" without documenting access patterns; rewrites half the data layer six months later.
antipattern.root-cause: Store-class chosen by trend, not by access pattern.
antipattern.fix: Default to Postgres + JSONB; only adopt NoSQL when an access pattern in the register explicitly justifies it.

antipattern.symptom: User document accumulates every event in a `history` array; document exceeds 16MB and writes start failing.
antipattern.root-cause: Embed verdict applied to an unbounded relationship.
antipattern.fix: Reference instead; cap bounded arrays with a documented growth ceiling (e.g. max 50 last_logins).

antipattern.symptom: Redis maxmemory hit on Friday night; eviction policy random-evicts session keys; users force-logged-out at scale.
antipattern.root-cause: TTL policy never authored; raw keys without expiry.
antipattern.fix: Author ttl_policy section before launch; pre-commit validator rejects any prefix with `ttl_seconds=0` or missing.

antipattern.symptom: Single Cassandra node CPU at 100%; rest of cluster idle; writes stall.
antipattern.root-cause: Partition key = `sensor_id` for a time-series; one heavy sensor concentrates writes.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `(sensor_id, time_bucket)` partition key; distributes writes across nodes; document the choice in `partition_key.primary_query`.
